---
title: "Quarto Dual Output Example"
subtitle: "One source file rendered to PDF or Reveal.js"
author: "Your Name"
date: today
format:
  pdf:
    toc: true
    number-sections: true
    colorlinks: true
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    geometry:
      - margin=1in
  revealjs:
    theme: [default, ../themes/thesis.scss]
    slide-number: c/t
    controls: true
    progress: true
    transition: fade
    footer: "Quarto dual output example"
execute:
  echo: false
---

# Purpose

This file demonstrates a practical Quarto pattern: keep the source in one
`.qmd` file and render it to different output formats.

Render PDF:

```bash
quarto render examples/dual-output.qmd --to pdf
```

Render Reveal.js HTML:

```bash
quarto render examples/dual-output.qmd --to revealjs
```

# Basic Markdown

Quarto documents are mostly Markdown with YAML metadata. The same content can
include prose, lists, tables, figures, math, citations, and code blocks.

## Lists

- Install Quarto.
- Install TinyTeX for PDF output.
- Write `.qmd` source.
- Render with `quarto render`.

## Table

| Output | Command | Main dependency |
|---|---|---|
| PDF | `quarto render file.qmd --to pdf` | TinyTeX or another TeX distribution |
| Reveal.js | `quarto render file.qmd --to revealjs` | Quarto |
| HTML | `quarto render file.qmd --to html` | Quarto |

# Figure

![A Quarto rendering pipeline generated from PlantUML source.](../diagrams/quarto-flow.svg){#fig-flow fig-alt="A flow diagram from QMD source through Quarto to HTML slides and PDF."}

Figure @fig-flow is kept as SVG so it stays sharp in both HTML and PDF output.

# Math

Inline math uses `$...$`, for example $E = mc^2$.

Display math:

$$
\hat{\theta} = \arg\min_\theta \sum_{i=1}^{n}(y_i - f_\theta(x_i))^2
$$

# Code

Static code blocks work in both PDF and Reveal.js:

```python
from pathlib import Path

source = Path("slides/slide-styles.qmd")
print(source.suffix)
```

# Callout

::: {.callout-note}
## Keep the render command explicit

Use `--to pdf` or `--to revealjs` when a document declares multiple formats.
That makes the intended output unambiguous.
:::

# Next Step

Use `slides/slide-styles.qmd` when you want a Reveal.js-specific gallery of
slide layouts, backgrounds, fragments, notes, code, callouts, and tabsets.
